title: podman run -t sends control characters to the terminal
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST?**:

[//]: **kind bug**

**Description**

```podman run```'s ```-t``` flag behaves differently than ```docker run```'s ```-t``` flag, in that it sends control characters to the output. If you use another program to run podman and collect the output from it to print to that controlling program's terminal, this will damage the controlling process's terminal. I have observed this problem with [bodhi-ci](https://github.com/fedora-infra/bodhi/blob/3fdbbb4d69e31e25163ad5612257a16b7902f0fc/devel/ci/bodhi-ci), which launches ```podman``` via Python's ```asyncio.create_subprocess_exec()``` and collects the output via a ```subprocess.PIPE```. It later prints the output to the terminal, but the control characters break the terminal (```reset``` must be used to get it functional again). ```bodhi-ci``` also supports ```docker```, and these control characters are not sent in that mode.

As a workaround, I plan to filter the output from ```podman``` to remove control characters, but I thought it would be worth reporting upstream in case ```podman``` wanted to try to emulate ```docker``` here as well.

Honestly, I could see an argument for keeping the current behaviour because ```podman run``` is able to do things like detect the width of the terminal when run directly in a terminal, but I thought it was worth filing about in case the discrepancy between docker and podman here was not desired. docker calls the ```--tty``` flags a "pseudo terminal", which I suppose might be related to why they don't use control characters. Perhaps podman could have both options, something like a ```--tty``` and a ```--real-tty```?

comments.body: I'm going to drop the bug label on this, but as you said, I think there is an argument to keep our current behavior. Adding an extra flag (`--pseudo-tty` or similar?) to toggle off sending control characters doesn't sound that difficult, but I'll have to check where we're generating them - I'm hopeful it's just our raw-TTY setup code, but it would be very hard to fix if conmon is generating them.
Sure thing - as said, I'm mostly reporting so the project is aware of the difference - it's up to you if you want to do anything in response or just close it ☺
@mheon, did you find time to check if it's the setup code or conmon?

I think it's us, not conmon, but it's been awhile so not 100% sure.

On Tue, Nov 20, 2018, 07:52 Valentin Rothberg <notifications@github.com
wrote:

> @mheon <https://github.com/mheon>, did you find time to check if it's the
> setup code or conmon?
>
> —
> You are receiving this because you were mentioned.
> Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub
> <https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/1671#issuecomment-440262833>,
> or mute the thread
> <https://github.com/notifications/unsubscribe-auth/AHYHCBPuGG82q1ISpnAOE5_w-qgY3dCfks5uw_sTgaJpZM4Xkuc0>
> .
>
@mtrmac @vrothberg Any update on this,  Can we close this issue?
@mheon ^
I think we should keep this open. This hasn't become an urgent issue yet, but it's also definitely not fixed.

BTW it’s not _obviously correct_ to drop control characters: if the container is provided a PTY slave FD, it will reasonably expect that it can actually treat it as a TTY — i.e. use syscalls to ask about the window size, and use terminal control sequences to control the terminal.  Basically, consider a `vi` container — that should be possible in principle, shouldn’t it?

Also, to avoid confusion, “pseudo TTY” just means “a TTY-like object without a physical device (like a serial port) underlying it”; every xterm window is backed by a pseudo-TTY master/slave pair.  It has nothing at all to do with stripping/not stripping control characters.

(To be fair, I haven’t even found the code in `docker run` that strips control characters, so I really have no opinion on the actual Docker/podman behavior difference.)
Shouldn't we be checking if we have a tty and then not send the control chars?  Or am I misunderstanding what is going on?
I think we want to leave this configurable if nothing else - I kind of like having control characters interpreted in some cases.
@mheon What should we do with this issue?
I think adding a flag to handle this is a nice-to-have, but not really a priority unless people start encountering issues with out TTY handling.
@mheon @baude Should we just close this?
Ummm. I think we should leave it open... It could end up affecting compatibility for some applications?
Added labels to get the attention of the community
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.
Don't see any reason to close this one.

@mheon I guess I have faced the same issue on "cockpit-podman". I see "^L" symbols when I am running podman container with a terminal. ``` [root@fc ~]# rpm -qa | grep podman cockpit-podman-10-1.fc31.noarch podman-1.6.2-2.fc31.x86_64 podman-manpages-1.6.2-2.fc31.noarch [root@fc ~]# ```
@mheon any chance your recent work happened to fix this?
Nope.
Will this continue to happen on APIV2?
Most likely yes, given the way our copying works, but I'm not 100% certain.
@ryanchpowell Could you work with @haircommander To fix this one.
Yes I can work on it

For reference, I don't know if I consider this one high priority. I think the cockpit errors are probably implementation details on their part, and nobody else has complained about this in two years...
But I believe we should be doing a tty check on stdout, and it it is not a real TTY we should drop the control characters, which I believe is what Docker is doing.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ryanchpowell Any movement on this?
I have not gotten to this yet, but can give it higher priority.
Are we sure this works differently on Docker?
Two year old issue, and it looks like it is still with us.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am going to close since this is the only time we have ever hear about this issue over many years.
